title: DPP and Cross-Lane Pattern Catalog from 2660 Production Kernels

row_newbcast.description: Broadcast value from lane N within a 16-lane row to all lanes in the row

pattern.instruction: v_mul_f32_dpp dst, scale_reg, mfma_result row_newbcast:N row_mask:0xf bank_mask:0xf
pattern.explanation: MFMA produces 16 results per row. Each row needs a different scale factor. row_newbcast:N takes the value from lane N in the source row and broadcasts it to all 16 lanes, so all lanes in a row get the same scale. This avoids LDS entirely for intra-wavefront scale distribution.
typical_sequence: ...(compute scale in v118 from loaded scale factors)...
v_add_f32_dpp v128, v118, v180 row_newbcast:0
v_add_f32_dpp v129, v118, v181 row_newbcast:1
v_add_f32_dpp v130, v118, v182 row_newbcast:2
v_add_f32_dpp v131, v118, v183 row_newbcast:3
pattern.why_fast: Without DPP: ds_write scale -> s_barrier -> ds_read scale (LDS round-trip = 20-40 cycles). With DPP: single cycle for broadcast within wavefront. Saves ~38 cycles per scale application.

quad_perm.description: Arbitrary permutation within each 4-lane quad (lanes 0-3, 4-7, 8-11, 12-15 of each row)
quad_perm.primary_use: Softmax derivative computation in FMHA backward pass

pattern.explanation: quad_perm:[0,0,0,0] broadcasts lane 0 to all 4 lanes in each quad. [1,0,3,2] swaps pairs. Used for computing dSoftmax = P * (dP - rowsum(P*dP)) where the rowsum needs to be broadcast across the quad.
typical_sequence: v_mov_b32_dpp v127, v124 quad_perm:[3,3,3,3]  // broadcast lane 3 of each quad
v_mov_b32_dpp v126, v124 quad_perm:[2,2,2,2]  // broadcast lane 2
v_mov_b32_dpp v125, v124 quad_perm:[1,1,1,1]  // broadcast lane 1
v_mov_b32_dpp v124, v124 quad_perm:[0,0,0,0]  // broadcast lane 0
// Then use these broadcast values for per-element softmax gradient:
v_subrev_f32_dpp v72, v150, v72 quad_perm:[0,0,0,0]
v_subrev_f32_dpp v73, v150, v73 quad_perm:[1,1,1,1]
pattern.why_fast: Replaces 4 LDS writes + barrier + 4 LDS reads (per quad) with direct register-level shuffle. Saves ~100 cycles per softmax gradient computation.

row_shr.description: Shift lanes right by N positions within a 16-lane row

pattern.explanation: Butterfly reduction pattern: shift by 1,2,4,8 and accumulate. After 4 steps, every lane in a 16-lane row has the reduction result.
typical_sequence_prefix_sum: v_mov_b32_dpp v31, v7 row_shr:1   // get value from lane+1
v_cndmask_b32 v31, 0, v31, vcc     // mask: only lanes > 0 participate
v_mov_b32_dpp v31, v7 row_shr:2   // get value from lane+2
v_mov_b32_dpp v31, v7 row_shr:4   // get value from lane+4
v_mov_b32_dpp v31, v7 row_shr:8   // get value from lane+8
v_add_u32 v7, v7, v31              // now v7 has prefix sum within 16-lane row
typical_sequence_reduction_layernorm: v_mov_b32_dpp v208, v16 row_ror:8  // rotate by 8
v_add_f32 v16, v208, v16           // sum with rotated value
v_add_f32 v16, v208, v16           // all 16 lanes have same sum
v_rsq_f32 v14, v14                 // reciprocal sqrt for normalization

row_shl.description: Shift lanes left by N positions within a 16-lane row
row_shl.primary_use: Parallel prefix OR / popcount for topk sorting

pattern.instruction: v_or_b32_dpp v7, v8, v8 row_shl:1 bound_ctrl:1
pattern.explanation: Parallel prefix OR: shift left and OR for bitmask propagation. bound_ctrl:1 prevents wrapping (out-of-range lanes get 0). Used for bitonic sort within wavefront.

row_bcast.description: Broadcast lane 15 or 31 to subsequent rows for cross-row communication
row_bcast.primary_use: Cross-row reduction step in topk (after intra-row reduction via row_shr)

typical_sequence: // After intra-row reduction with row_shr:1,2,4,8:
v_mov_b32_dpp v30, v7 row_bcast:15  // broadcast row0.lane15 to row1
v_add_u32 v7, v7, v30
v_mov_b32_dpp v30, v7 row_bcast:31  // broadcast row1.lane15 to rows 2-3
v_add_u32 v7, v7, v30               // now all 64 lanes have full sum

row_mirror.description: Mirror lane order within each row
row_mirror.primary_use: Used with wave_shl for full wavefront reduction

v_readlane_b32.description: Read specific VGPR lane into SGPR
v_readlane_b32.primary_use: Extract wavefront reduction results into scalar registers

typical_sequence: // In layernorm: extract partial sums from different lanes into SGPRs
v_readlane_b32 s9, v235, 1    // read lane 1 -> s9
v_readlane_b32 s8, v235, 0    // read lane 0 -> s8
// ... repeat for lanes 2-15
// Then do scalar arithmetic to combine partial sums

v_readfirstlane_b32.description: Read lane 0 of VGPR into SGPR
v_readfirstlane_b32.primary_use: Get uniform values (workgroup ID, thread count) into scalar path

ds_bpermute_b32.description: Arbitrary cross-lane permute within workgroup via LDS hardware
ds_bpermute_b32.primary_use: Distribute computed scale factors to arbitrary lanes in paged attention

typical_sequence: // Compute per-token quantization scale via max reduction:
v_max3_f32 v92, |v8|, |v9|, v92     // tree max across registers
ds_write_b32 v118, v92 offset:1280   // write local max to LDS
s_barrier                             // sync workgroup
ds_read_b32 v76, v119 offset:1280    // read other lanes' maxes
// ... reduce across lanes ...
v_rcp_f32 v92, v92                    // reciprocal of global max
// Compute target lane index:
v_lshlrev_b32 v74, 2, v74            // lane * 4 (byte address)
ds_bpermute_b32 v72, v74, v92         // send scale to target lane
s_waitcnt lgkmcnt(0)
v_mul_f32 v8, v8, v72                 // apply scale
v_cvt_pk_fp8_f32 v8, v8, v9           // convert to FP8

key_techniques: AGPRs for MFMA operands, VGPRs for accumulation
v_perm_b32 for BF16 byte layout transformation
gemm_bf16.scheduling_pattern: 48 MFMAs interleaved with 40 buffer_loads and 28 ds_reads per loop iteration

key_techniques: DPP row_newbcast for per-block dequantization (avoids LDS for scale distribution)
v_pk_fma_f32 for packed FP32 FMA with scale
DPP quad_perm for intra-quad value broadcast (softmax derivative)
Multiple s_barrier pairs for ping-pong double buffering
v_max3_f32 tree reduction for dynamic quantization range
Heavy s_barrier usage (40+ per kernel) for workgroup sync
DPP row_ror:8,4,2,1 butterfly reduction for wavefront-level sum
v_readlane_b32 for extracting partial sums to SGPRs

topk_sorting.dpp_type: row_shr/shl for prefix scan, row_bcast for cross-row
key_techniques: DPP row_shr:1,2,4,8 for prefix sum within 16-lane row
DPP wave_shl:1 + row_mirror for full wavefront reduction
v_cndmask_b32 for conditional participation in reduction
Same DPP row_newbcast pattern as FP8 GEMM
Fused GEMM + quantization in single kernel
v_max3_f32 tree reduction for output quantization

lds_when_dpp_possible.description: Using LDS write + barrier + LDS read for intra-wavefront communication when DPP can do it in 1 cycle
lds_when_dpp_possible.symptom: ds_write -> s_barrier -> ds_read pattern for values that only need broadcast within a wavefront
lds_when_dpp_possible.fix: Replace with v_*_dpp using row_newbcast or quad_perm
lds_when_dpp_possible.cycle_savings: 20-40 cycles per occurrence (LDS latency + barrier cost)

scalar_loads_instead_of_vectorized.description: Using buffer_load_dword x4 instead of buffer_load_dwordx4
scalar_loads_instead_of_vectorized.symptom: 4 consecutive scalar loads to contiguous addresses

full_wait_instead_of_partial.description: Using vmcnt(0) when only the most recent load is needed
full_wait_instead_of_partial.symptom: s_waitcnt vmcnt(0) with many loads in flight
full_wait_instead_of_partial.fix: Use vmcnt(N-1) where N is loads in flight
full_wait_instead_of_partial.cycle_savings: Proportional to load latency hiding opportunity

reduction_via_lds_within_wavefront.description: Using LDS for wavefront-level reductions instead of DPP butterfly
reduction_via_lds_within_wavefront.symptom: ds_write_b32 + s_barrier + ds_read_b32 x16 for reduction within single wavefront

wavefront_synchronization.implicit_sync: All lanes in a wavefront execute in lockstep (SIMT). No explicit sync needed within a single wavefront.
wavefront_synchronization.s_barrier: Synchronizes all wavefronts within a workgroup. Required before reading LDS written by other wavefronts.
wavefront_synchronization.s_waitcnt: Waits for outstanding async operations (VMEM loads, LDS ops, exports). Not a sync primitive per se, but ensures data availability.
wavefront_synchronization.ds_bpermute: Implicit LDS hardware sync - waits for the permute to complete (appears as lgkmcnt dependency).
wavefront_synchronization.key_insight: DPP operates within a single wavefront and requires NO synchronization. LDS operations between wavefronts require s_barrier. This is why DPP is preferred for intra-wavefront communication.
